results.title: RightAngle Developer | Hirevector
highlights:
RightAngle Developer | Hirevector
[...]
# RightAngle Developer
[...]
Posted May 28, 2026
[...]
#WeAreONEOK – Fortune 500 company. 100+ years in business. Leading midstream service provider. Safety first. Sustainable operations. Environmentally responsible. Employee focused.
[...]
ONEOK is seeking a RightAngle Technical Analyst/Developer to support and enhance our commercial trading and logistics systems. This role is responsible for developing, maintaining, and supporting multiple instances of the RightAngle ETRM application, enabling critical business processes across Natural Gas Liquids (NGL), Refined Products, and Crude Oil commercial operations.
[...]
The successful candidate will work closely with application development teams, the Business Process Optimization team, and key business partners to deliver scalable, reliable, and high‑quality technology solutions that support ONEOK’s commercial objectives.
[...]
Job Responsibilities
[...]
- Design, develop, configure, and support the RightAngle application across multiple environments

- Analyze business requirements and translate them into technical solutions aligned with ONEOK standards

- Collaborate with developers, business analysts, and business stakeholders to enhance commercial workflows

- Develop and maintain custom RightAngle components, integrations, reports, and batch processes

- Support day‑to‑day production operations, including incident resolution, root cause analysis, and performance tuning

- Participate in system upgrades, patches, and new module implementations

- Ensure application solutions align with data, security, and compliance standards

- Create and maintain technical documentation, including design specifications and support procedures

- Provide technical guidance and mentoring within the development team as needed
[...]
- Experience supporting energy trading systems within NGL, Refined Products, or Crude Oil commercial operations

- Experience with RightAngle API’s, scheduling, pricing, or settlements modules

- Knowledge of Lease and ATM (Asset Transaction Management) processes is a plus

- Familiarity with system integrations, batch processing, and ETL workflows

- Experience working in Agile or iterative development environments

- Prior experience collaborating with business process optimization or transformation teams
[...]
- Bachelor's Degree in MIS, computer science, mathematics, or other related field, or an equivalent combination of formal education and the following job related experience
[...]
- Experience with multiple development languages and/or platforms

- Experience with software development and programming concepts/techniques

- Experience with database management systems

- Experience with all aspects of the software development lifecycle methodology and basic project management concepts

- Experience with basic infrastructure concepts and how those concepts are impacted by development activities
[...]
Knowledge, Skills and Abilities
[...]
- Skills in: use and function of office equipment including computers and applicable software

- Ability to: apply math, algebra, and statistical methods.

- Ability to: interact, advise and communicate effectively

- Ability to: develop information, conduct meetings and make presentations

- Ability to: read and write fluently in English

- Ability to: communicate and/or exchange information or instruction; conduct oral presentations and/or meetings
[...]
Expected Salary Range
[...]
$102,000.00 - $154,000.00

results.title: AI Machine Learning Engineer at Occidental Petroleum | Jeremy
highlights:
AI Machine Learning Engineer at Occidental Petroleum | Jeremy

Skip
[...]
AI Machine Learning Engineer at Occidental Petroleum | Jeremy
[...]
# AI Machine Learning Engineer
3 days agoHouston, TexasMid-LevelSeniorML / AI
[...]
LLMAIDeep LearningRelational DatabasesCloudModel TrainingData EngineeringCausal Inference
[...]
Research and build AI/ML systems for oil and gas applications including subsurface characterization and reservoir engineering at Occidental Petroleum's AI Center of Excellence in Houston.
[...]
## Full Job Description
[...]
We are looking for an experienced and innovative individual contributor to fill the position of **AI Machine Learning Engineer **within our **AI Center of Excellence** group based in **Houston, TX. **The incumbent will research, build, and design artificial intelligence systems to automate predictive models, and design machine learning systems, models and schemes. 
**Essential Job Responsibilities**
[...]
* Work with Subject Matter Experts to design appropriate machine learning model types and architectures for particular applications ranging from subsurface characterization, reservoir engineering, production, and facilities etc
* Tune algorithm hyper-parameters and perform model training and validation
* Create workflows to optimize performance for computationally heavy workloads
* Collaborate with Machine Learning Operation team to deploy machine learning solutions to the cloud
[...]
**Qualifications**
[...]
* Bachelor's degree in computer science, mathematics, physics, engineering or related field required
* Master's degree is highly preferred. Equivalent combination of advanced education and relevant experience will be considered.
* A minimum of 5 years of experience and deep knowledge in orchestration methods and tools to optimally use computational infrastructure, and machine learning methods and algorithms
* Strong Python or R programming skills required
* Experience developing, architecting, and running ML or deep learning workload in the Cloud.
* Ability to express the intuition behind basic ML algorithms
* Experience performing basic hyper parameter optimization
* Ability to follow model training, deployment, and operational best practices
* Experience with Docker for containerization and Kubernetes for orchestration
* Knowledge of Terraform and Ansible for automating infrastructure management
* Knowledge of YAML for automating model and application code deployment
* Understanding of data engineering principles and databases (relational, NoSQL)
* Strong critical thinking and communication skills
**Additional Desired Qualifications:**
[...]
* AWS certification is highly preferred, with a strong preference for the AWS Certified Machine Learning – Specialty credential.
* Experience with Bayesian statistical modeling, including Bayesian inference, Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC), and causal inference.
* Hands‑on experience in geospatial data analytics using Esri products, with a focus on ArcGIS Enterprise.
* Experience with LLM-powered applications such as chatbots, question-answering systems, document processing and agentic workflow design.
[...]
Apply on Occidental PetroleumView Original

results.title: Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
highlights:
Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
[...]
# Data Scientist
[...]
Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. Imagine working with massive volumes of healthcare data spanning clinical, claims, behavioral, and digital interactions – powering intelligent personalization, discovery, and decision support for millions of members, providers, and care teams.
[...]
At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.
[...]
Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.
[...]
- Design, develop, and deploy advanced analytics, machine learning, and Generative AI solutions to address complex healthcare and business problems using largescale structured and unstructured data
[...]
- Applies advanced techniques including statistics, machine learning, simulation, and mathematical modelling
[...]
- Build and optimize LLM based applications, including Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks, to enable accurate, context aware, and explainable insights from enterprise healthcare data sources such as clinical notes, claims, provider data, and policy documents
[...]
- Develop, evaluate, and finetune machine learning and GenAI models, including prompt engineering, retrieval strategies, and model evaluation techniques to improve accuracy, relevance, and performance
[...]
- Translate complex analytical and GenAI outputs into clear, actionable insights for business stakeholders, product teams, and
[...]
to support data driven decision
[...]
- Support prototyping, validation, and deployment of GenAI and ML solutions, working with engineering teams to ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable. Continuously stay current with advancements in LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and healthcare AI trends, contributing innovative ideas and improvements to Optum’s capabilities
[...]
### Required Qualifications:
[...]
- Bachelor’s Degree (preferably in information technology, engineering, math, computer science, analytics, engineering or other related field)
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in handling/active development in data science projects
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in Microsoft Azure Cloud, Databricks, Mlflow, Spark SQL & Python
[...]
- Experience using Machine Learning Algorithms: Linear & Logistic Regression, Time Series analysis, Support Vector Machine, K-Means clustering, K-Nearest Neighbors Decision Trees, Random Forest, Naive Bayes, PCA, SVD, Artificial Neural Networks, Association Rules, Genetic Algorithm, Optimization, Bagging and Boosting algorithms etc.
[...]
- Understanding of data, schema, data model, machine learning and how to bring efficiency in big data related life cycle
[...]
- Proven skills to create opportunities using machine learning or artificial intelligence
[...]
Optum, part of UnitedHealth
[...]
Prairie, Minnesota, Optum serves patients, providers, payers, and pharmacies worldwide with innovative platforms for care optimization, claims processing, and population health management.

Title: AI/ML / Generative AI Engineer

Employer Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. Location Tarrytown, New York Start date May 23, 2026

Regenerons Data Enablement and Analytics (DEA) team within Preclinical Manufacturing and Process Development (PMPD) is seeking a Machine Learning Engineer to develop, build and deploy analyses and machine learning models that support bioprocess development. Regeneron is a leading biotechnology company that invents, develops, and commercializes life-transforming medicines for people with serious diseases. Founded and led by physician-scientists, our unique ability to repeatedly and consistently translate science into medicine has led to numerous approved treatments and product candidates in development, most of which were homegrown in our laboratories. Our medicines and pipeline are designed to help patients with eye diseases, allergic and inflammatory diseases, cancer, cardiovascular and metabolic diseases, neurological diseases, hematologic conditions, infectious diseases, and rare diseases.

Biomedical AI/ML Engineer responsible for developing and maintaining production-level software tools, implementing AI/ML solutions, and collaborating with scientific and IT personnel. Position Overview And ResponsibilitiesThe Center for Biomedical Data Sciences is seeking a Biomedical AI/ML Engineer to support OMRF AI initiatives. This role will work collaboratively with the Associate Director for AI and Research Computing Initiatives and Information Technology to develop, manage, and facilitate effective adoption of internal AI/ML solutions. This position is ideal for an individual passionate about applications of AI/ML in biomedical research and helping people from a wide variety of backgrounds learn how to use these rapidly evolving technologies.Some Key Responsibilities Include
[...]
- Developing and maintaining production-level software tools for processing and analyzing complex biomedical data.
- Implementing and deploying Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines for querying vector databases, relational databases, and graph databases, as well as agentic tool execution.
- Integrating technologies, such as RAG or tool execution, into chat interfaces.
- Collaborate with scientific and IT personnel to identify AI/ML use cases which are both valuable and scalable.
- Work both independently and as part of the broader CBDS/IT team, contributing to OMRF AI initiatives and training personnel on using AI effectively.

- Knowledge of computer programming (e.g., R and Python)

- Strong organizational and communication skills, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a research setting.

- s degree in Computer Science, Bioinformatics, Data Science, Mathematics, Biostatistics, or a related field with at least two (2) years of relevant experience.

- Expertise in DevOps/MLOps/AIOps and developing reproducible code.
- Experience with containerization (e.g., using Docker or Podman) and container management (e.g., Kubernetes).

results.title: Scientific Lead, Generative AI Engineer, Applied Intelligence for Discovery job with Eli Lilly and Company | 3049014
highlights:
Scientific Lead, Generative AI Engineer, Applied Intelligence for Discovery job with Eli Lilly and Company | 3049014
[...]
# Scientific Lead, Generative AI Engineer, Applied Intelligence for Discovery
[...]
Employer Eli Lilly and Company Location San Francisco, California Start date May 15, 2026
[...]
## Job Details
[...]
We are building something unprecedented, an AI foundation that will fundamentally change how drug discovery research is conducted.
[...]
The Applied Intelligence for Discovery (AI4D) team is a newly formed group within Lilly Research Laboratories that operates at the intersection of scientific delivery and core platform development. AI4D’s mission is to connecting scientists to petabyte-scale data through natural language interfaces, automated analysis workflows, and intelligent search — and to convert early deployments into repeatable system standards and evaluation practices that scale across therapeutic areas.
[...]
As a Generative AI Engineer, you will design, build, and operate the core AI systems that power this transformation: retrieval-augmented generation over internal scientific documents, text-to-SQL over complex omics databases, agentic workflows that automate multi-step analyses, and the evaluation infrastructure that able the next-generation of medicines for patients.
[...]
Key Responsibilities
[...]
Design, build, and optimize RAG pipelines over internal publications, study reports, electronic lab notebooks, and other scientific documents
[...]
Build hybrid retrieval systems combining vector search with structured metadata, knowledge graphs, and ontology-aware filtering
[...]
Build and optimize text-to-SQL systems over Lilly’s databases, enabling scientists to query gene expression, proteomics, pathway, and variant data through natural language
[...]
Develop schema documentation, semantic annotations, and gold-standard question/SQL pairs that bridge how scientists think about data and how it is stored
[...]
Implement multi-step reasoning approaches (chain-of-thought, self-correction, Reflexion loops) to improve accuracy on complex scientific queries
[...]
Design agentic AI workflows that chain database queries, bioinformatics tools, literature search, and visualization into automated multi-step scientific analyses
[...]
Evaluate and integrate emerging orchestration frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, custom architectures) for scientific use cases
[...]
Build evaluation frameworks measuring accuracy, reliability, and scientific validity of AI outputs
[...]
PhD in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related technical field with 0-3+ years of experience; or equivalent experience building production LLM systems; MS in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related technical field with 5+ years of experience; or equivalent experience building production LLM systems
[...]
Experience building LLM-powered applications, including at least two of: RAG systems, text-to-SQL, agentic workflows, or fine-tuning pipelines
[...]
Strong software engineering skills in Python with experience building production-grade systems
[...]
Deep familiarity with the modern LLM ecosystem: embedding models, vector databases, and orchestration frameworks
[...]
Experience designing evaluation frameworks for LLM systems — systematic approaches to measuring accuracy, detecting hallucinations, and tracking regressions
[...]
Comfort working with complex, heterogeneous data — databases with hundreds of tables, specialized schemas, or domain-specific vocabularies
[...]
Familiarity with biomedical data types (omics, clinical, molecular) or scientific databases
[...]
Experience with MLOps/LLMOps tooling: experiment tracking, model registries, prompt versioning, A/B testing for AI systems
[...]
Knowledge of biomedical ontologies (Gene Ontology, MeSH, ChEBI) or experience integrating domain-specific knowledge into LLM systems
[...]
Actual compensation will depend on a
[...]
’s education,
[...]
$181,500 - $283,800

results.title: Senior Director/ VP, AI at OKX — Hong Kong | SailOnchain
highlights:
Senior Director/ VP, AI at OKX — Hong Kong | SailOnchain
[...]
# Senior Director/ VP, AI - OKX
[...]
## About the Opportunity
[...]
OKX is looking for a Senior Director of AI to lead both the people and the product — building and managing a high-calibre AI team while maintaining close involvement in how autonomous agent systems are designed, evaluated, and shipped into production. This role is for someone who leads by doing: grounded in the technical craft, sharp on product direction, and able to translate both into real business outcomes.
[...]
## What You’ll Be Doing
[...]
- Team Leadership — Build, manage, and develop a team of AI engineers and researchers; own hiring, growth, and performance across the function
[...]
- Agent Product Delivery — Lead end-to-end delivery of autonomous AI Agent products, covering the full lifecycle from requirements and architecture through to production operations
[...]
- Eval-Driven Development — Establish benchmark-driven evaluation frameworks that replace manual iteration with quantifiable quality signals across agent systems
[...]
- Agent Harness Design — Design and implement end-to-end Agent Harness infrastructure to ensure agent stability and reliability in complex, real-world scenarios
[...]
- LLMOrchestration — Orchestrate creative reasoning steps alongside deterministic guardrails, finding the right balance between flexibility and control
[...]
- Engineering Workflow Integration — Embed AI Agent capabilities deeply into core products and engineering pipelines, improving both developer productivity and business value
[...]
- Cross-functional Alignment — Collaborate with product, engineering, and business teams to ensure technical direction stays tightly aligned with strategic priorities
[...]
## What We Look For In You
[...]
- Master's or PhD in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field
- 10+ years of experience in AI/ML research, productization, or platform development, including core technical or leadership roles at globally recognized technology companies
[...]
- Deep expertise in LLMs and Generative AI, deep learning, reinforcement learning, recommendation systems, and related areas
[...]
- Proven track record of translating AI capabilities into measurable business outcomes
[...]
- End-to-end delivery experience with autonomous AI Agent products, from inception and architecture through to production operations
[...]
- Hands-on experience integrating AI deeply into engineering and development workflows — such as AI coding assistants, automated code review, or developer productivity tooling
[...]
## Nice to Haves
[...]
- Background at an AI-native startup or frontier AI lab, with familiarity with fast-cycle product iteration
- AI publications, patents, or industry-recognized technical contributions
- Familiarity with the unique technical challenges of applying AI in crypto, fintech, or high-frequency data environments

• Ship production-grade AI agents and automation workflows that solve high-stakes business problems in cross-functional orgs like Finance, Legal, and People. • Collaborate shoulder-to-shoulder with Subject Matter Experts to translate manual business processes into performant, reliable technical specifications and agentic architectures. • Rapidly prototype and deploy end-to-end solutions using Python and modern AI frameworks (LLM APIs, RAG patterns, and agentic SDKs), with clear ownership from discovery through production. • Build and maintain robust integrations between OnePay’s core SaaS stack (Salesforce, Workday, etc.) and custom AI services, ensuring data integrity and security. • Empower and educate internal partners, acting as a technical force multiplier to help non-technical teams adopt AI-first mindsets and build their own automated agents. • Instrument monitoring and guardrails to ensure all AI-driven workflows are secure, compliant, and resilient at scale — including appropriate handling of PII, access control design, audit logging, and coordination with Legal and Security review processes. • Design and run evaluation frameworks to validate agent accuracy, reliability, and regression behavior before and after production deployments. • Lead structured discovery sessions with business stakeholders to surface requirements, assess feasibility, and produce clear technical scopes before a single line of code is written. • Create reusable playbooks, architecture decision records, and handoff documentation so that the teams you work with can maintain and extend what you build.

results.title: Sr Data Scientist, Compliance - OKX | Hirevector
highlights:
Sr Data Scientist, Compliance - OKX | Hirevector
[...]
# Sr Data Scientist, Compliance - OKX
[...]
This is a role for someone who finds
[...]
genuinely interesting.
[...]
global crypto exchange
[...]
data landscape is more varied and more analytically demanding
[...]
: on-chain transaction data, cross-jurisdictional exposure, emerging typologies, and a regulatory environment that is still being shaped.
[...]
- You will join a team of data scientists, ML engineers, and analytics engineers working closely with compliance specialists across AML, sanctions, KYC/KYB, and transaction monitoring. The role offers a good balance between deep technical work and meaningful engagement with the domain experts who understand what the outputs need to achieve. It suits someone who is comfortable moving between a complex modelling problem and a conversation with a compliance analyst about what a result actually means.
[...]
- AI-assisted analysis is already part of how this team works. We use LLM tooling to accelerate coding and investigation workflows, automated pipelines to replace repetitive analytical tasks, and AI-augmented approaches to surface patterns faster. We are looking for data scientists who already work this way and who can bring that fluency to hard compliance problems, helping the team do better work rather than learning the basics on the job.
[...]
- Develop and validate analytical models and detection strategies for compliance use cases including AML typology detection, customer risk rating, KYC/KYB risk scoring, transaction monitoring calibration, and SAR analytics, working closely with ML engineers on feature design and production implementation.
[...]
- Conduct exploratory analysis on on-chain and off-chain datasets to surface patterns, validate hypotheses, and develop candidate features for detection models, using AI-assisted tooling to accelerate the cycle from hypothesis to validated insight.
[...]
- Apply AI-assisted coding and automation as standard practice: using LLM tools to write, review, and improve analytical code; building automated workflows that reduce manual query cycles; and contributing to AI-augmented investigation tools that help compliance analysts work through alerts and cases more effectively.
[...]
- Work with ML engineers and data engineers to ensure
[...]
work is designed for production, reviewing feature logic, validating data quality assumptions, and providing the domain context that engineers need to
[...]
pipelines that behave correctly.
[...]
- Stay closely engaged with developments in compliance data science: graph ML, LLM-based investigation tools, anomaly detection, and evolving AML typologies in crypto and fiat environments, with an eye toward what is genuinely production-ready versus what is still experimental.
[...]
- 8+ years in data science, quantitative analytics, or a related field, with experience working on compliance, financial crime, or regulatory analytics problems in financial services, fintech, or a crypto exchange. We welcome candidates across seniority levels; scope and responsibilities will be aligned with your experience.
[...]
- Solid Python and SQL, with applied experience in machine learning across supervised, unsupervised, and anomaly detection methods, and a working knowledge of how those approaches translate to compliance problem formulations such as typology detection, risk scoring, and threshold calibration.
[...]
- Experience working with big data platforms such as Spark, Databricks, Hadoop, or MaxCompute, and comfort with both notebook-based exploratory work and structured analytical delivery.
[...]
- Hands-on fluency with AI-assisted working practices. You already use LLM coding tools as part of your regular workflow, have experience building or contributing to automated analytical pipelines, and have a practical view on how AI can improve the quality and speed of compliance analytics work. We are looking for people who have put this into practice.

As a Senior Associate, Generative AI Engineer in the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help design, build, and support production generative artificial intelligence products and reusable backend application programming interfaces used across the firm. You will combine large enterprise datasets with large language and multimodal models to deliver scalable, measurable solutions. results.title: Lead Generative AI Specialist | Zenvok
highlights:
Lead Generative AI Specialist | Zenvok
[...]
# Lead Generative AI Specialist
[...]
Are you passionate about improving capabilities, efficiency and performance? Are you interested in responsible for supporting the business owners to lean the process? Join our Digital Technology Team! Our team provides industry-leading products and services that optimize the production and processing of energy. You will contribute to our future as an AI player in the Energy Transformation industry. Partner with the best As a Generative AI Specialist, you will work in teams providing smart solution and addressing problems in a commercial technology and consultancy development environment. In this role, you will contribute to the development and deployment of modern generative AI models, techniques and operational research to leverage the power of data to solve complex problems and push the boundaries of what is possible in the ever-evolving tech landscape. As a Lead Generative AI Specialist, you will be responsible for: Designing and developing of generative AI models, including but not limited to LLMs, VLMs, GANs and Multimodal LLMs. Conducting and driving forward innovative research in the field of generative AI, leading the team in exploring new methodologies and technologies. Working closely with cross-functional teams to integrate new generative models into existing AI systems, ensuring seamless integration and optimizing performance. Developing and testing software components according to customer and technical requirements. Preparing comprehensive reports and presentations on the impact of generative AI initiatives on the business. Fuel your passion T o be successful in this role you will have: Master's degree in computer science, Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field with a specialization in generative models and proven experience in the development and deployment of generative AI models. PhD is preferred Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work across different disciplines. Proven experience in software programming languages, with a focus on Python, including AI libraries eg PyTorch, TensorFlow, Transformer architecture. Demonstrated skill at function in a team setting Demonstrated awareness of critical thinking and problem solving methods Demonstrated awareness of presentation and influencing skills Ability to analyze and resolve problems through innovation and creative solutions. Natural communication skills, with an ability to engage stakeholders at all levels. Self-motivation that promotes teamwork and collaboration in a cross-functional environment. Work in a way that works for you We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone too. In this role, we can offer the following flexible working patterns: Working remotely from home or any other work location Working with us Our people are at the heart of what we do at Baker Hughes. We know we are better when all of our people are developed, engaged and able to bring their whole authentic selves to work. We invest in the health and well-being of our workforce, train and reward talent and develop leaders at all levels to bring out the best in each other. Working for you Our inventions have revolutionized energy for over a century. But to keep going forward tomorrow, we know we have to push the boundaries today. We prioritize rewarding those who embrace change with a package that reflects how much we value their input. Join us, and you can expect: Contemporary work-life balance policies and wellbeing activities Comprehensive private medical care options Safety net of life insurance and disability programs Tailored financial programs Additional elected or voluntary benefits The Baker Hughes internal title for this role is: Lead Engineer, Mathematics & Data Science, Research and Technology Development

- 5+ years in data science or growth analytics (fintech/crypto preferred), with a demonstrated track record of moving metrics — not just reporting them.
[...]
AI Engineering Capability

- Strong Python; hands-on with LLM APIs, prompt engineering, RAG, and agentic frameworks; able to rapidly evaluate and integrate new AI tooling into production workflows.
[...]
Expert-level
[...]
; proficiency in Python for analysis
[...]
, and automation.
- Hands-on experience designing and analyzing
[...]
/B experiments; solid understanding of statistical
[...]
and experimentation best practices.
- Experience
[...]
dashboards, self-serve data tools, and automated pipelines; familiarity with predictive modeling, user segmentation, and cohort analysis.

results.title: Sr Data Scientist, Compliance - OKX | Quickswoop
highlights:
Sr Data Scientist, Compliance - OKX | Quickswoop
[...]
# Sr Data Scientist, Compliance - OKX
[...]
- This is a role for someone who finds
[...]
genuinely interesting. At a global crypto exchange, the compliance
[...]
landscape is more varied and more analytically demanding than in most
[...]
-chain transaction
[...]
environment that is still being shaped.
[...]
- You will join a team of data scientists, ML engineers, and analytics engineers working closely with compliance specialists across AML, sanctions, KYC/KYB, and transaction monitoring. The role offers a good balance between deep technical work and meaningful engagement with the domain experts who understand what the outputs need to achieve. It suits someone who is comfortable moving between a complex modelling problem and a conversation with a compliance analyst about what a result actually means.
[...]
- AI-assisted analysis is already part of how this team works. We use LLM tooling to accelerate coding and investigation workflows, automated pipelines to replace repetitive analytical tasks, and AI-augmented approaches to surface patterns faster. We are looking for data scientists who already work this way and who can bring that fluency to hard compliance problems, helping the team do better work rather than learning the basics on the job.
[...]
- Develop and validate analytical models and detection strategies for compliance use cases including AML typology detection, customer risk rating, KYC/KYB risk scoring, transaction monitoring calibration, and SAR analytics, working closely with ML engineers on feature design and production implementation.
[...]
- Conduct exploratory analysis on on-chain and off-chain datasets to surface patterns, validate hypotheses, and develop candidate features for detection models, using AI-assisted tooling to accelerate the cycle from hypothesis to validated insight.
[...]
- Apply AI-assisted coding and automation as standard practice: using LLM tools to write, review, and improve analytical code; building automated workflows that reduce manual query cycles; and contributing to AI-augmented investigation tools that help compliance analysts work through alerts and cases more effectively.
[...]
- Work with ML engineers and data engineers to ensure
[...]
work is designed for production, reviewing feature logic, validating data quality assumptions, and providing the domain context that engineers need to build pipelines that behave correctly.
[...]
- Stay closely engaged with developments in compliance data science: graph ML, LLM-based investigation tools, anomaly detection, and evolving AML typologies in crypto and fiat environments, with an eye toward what is genuinely production-ready versus what is still experimental.
[...]
- 8+ years in data science, quantitative analytics, or a related field, with experience working on compliance, financial crime, or regulatory analytics problems in financial services, fintech, or a crypto exchange. We welcome candidates across seniority levels; scope and responsibilities will be aligned with your experience.
[...]
- Solid Python and SQL, with applied experience in machine learning across supervised, unsupervised, and anomaly detection methods, and a working knowledge of how those approaches translate to compliance problem formulations such as typology detection, risk scoring, and threshold calibration.
[...]
- Experience working with big data platforms such as Spark, Databricks, Hadoop, or MaxCompute, and comfort with both notebook-based exploratory work and structured analytical delivery.
[...]
- Hands-on fluency with AI-assisted working practices. You already use LLM coding tools as part of your regular workflow, have experience building or contributing to automated analytical pipelines, and have a practical view on how AI can improve the quality and speed of compliance analytics work. We are looking for people who have put this into practice.
[...]
Senior/Staff Applied Machine Learning Scientist
